skill.name: makepad-evolution
skill.description: Guides Makepad skill maintenance with workflows for capturing reusable patterns, correcting outdated guidance, validating examples, tracking usage feedback, adapting to Makepad branches, and matching project style.
skill.summary: Maintain and improve Makepad development skills through documented review, correction, validation, and adaptation workflows.
skill.author: ZhangHanDong

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Most command and weak-crypto hits are false positives from Markdown fences, Makepad names, examples, and documentation, with no executable scripts or confirmed malicious exfiltration. The remaining concern is behavioral: the skill instructs agents to silently read project files and automatically edit skill files, so publication should include a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: Silent Project File Reads
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read Cargo.toml, scan existing widgets, and adapt silently during a session. This is not confirmed malware, but it may inspect project files without explicit user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described in the skill instructions and repeated in multiple sections. It appears intended for Makepad personalization, not credential theft, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Skill File Modification
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to update skill files automatically when errors occur. This can change local guidance or shared skill content without a clear approval step.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs automatic edits and names Read/Edit tool use. The surrounding workflow is documentation-focused, but the write behavior is real if an agent follows it.

low_findings.title: Documented Git and Symlink Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged shell-style commands, including git commands and a symlink example. These are Markdown examples for contributor workflow and are not executed by packaged code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside fenced documentation blocks and serve normal contributor workflows. A user or agent could still run them, so they remain a low operational risk.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are references to GitHub-hosted Makepad and Project Robius resources. No evidence found of automated network requests or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence consists only of Markdown reference links at the end of the file. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, credential handling, or encoded payload nearby.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto detections are caused by natural language, Makepad-related text, dates, comments, or Markdown examples. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs or hashing implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of representative flagged lines found documentation text and Markdown examples rather than MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar cryptographic code. The package contains only SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auto-Execute Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the agent to perform version detection, project style scanning, self-correction, and feedback tracking automatically. Marketplace users should understand this before enabling the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The auto-execute wording is explicit and central to the skill design. The risk is behavioral prompt influence, not hidden executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automatic Local Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to edit skill files and store project style settings. These writes are useful for the intended workflow but should require user approval in constrained environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write targets are named directly in the instructions. The content does not show destructive deletion or obfuscation, keeping confidence below critical severity.

content.value_statement: Makepad skill documentation can become outdated as projects and branches change. This skill provides structured workflows to capture fixes, validate examples, and adapt guidance to project style.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to capture reusable Makepad patterns, shader techniques, layout fixes, and troubleshooting notes.
Provides formats for adding pattern, shader, troubleshooting, validation, and usage feedback entries.
Documents branch-aware Makepad guidance for main, dev, and rik workflows.
Explains how to locate related Makepad skill files from the current skill directory.
Tracks pattern health with usage, success, failure, and validation markers.
Guides adaptation of generated Makepad code to existing project naming and structure.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include runnable validation tooling.
Automatic file scanning and edits depend on the host agent permissions.
Makepad API guidance may still require manual verification against current source.
It is designed for the makepad-skills repository layout, not arbitrary skill systems.

use_cases.title: Maintain Shared Makepad Guidance
use_cases.description: Capture a reusable widget, shader, or layout pattern after solving a real project problem.

use_cases.title: Correct Outdated Skill Advice
use_cases.description: Document a Makepad API correction after a suggested pattern fails to compile or run.

use_cases.title: Review Pattern Health
use_cases.description: Summarize which documented Makepad patterns succeed, fail, or need review after repeated use.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for adding a discovered Makepad pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: I found a reusable Makepad pattern in this project. Help me decide where it belongs and draft the documentation entry.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Bad Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for correcting inaccurate skill advice.
prompt_templates.prompt: A Makepad skill suggestion failed to compile. Identify the wrong guidance, explain the fix, and prepare a correction note.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Current Skills
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of examples and branch compatibility.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the Makepad skill guidance against my current branch and report outdated examples, risky advice, and suggested fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Skill Evolution
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance planning for a shared skill repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review recent Makepad skill changes, summarize pattern health, and propose a safe evolution plan for maintainers.

output_examples.input: I solved a recurring loading button problem in a Makepad app.
output_examples.output: A suggested documentation entry with a pattern title, problem statement, implementation summary, usage notes, and an evolution marker.

output_examples.input: The timer guidance from a skill no longer works on my branch.
output_examples.output: A correction summary that names the failing guidance, explains the branch-specific API change, and suggests a troubleshooting entry.

output_examples.input: Show pattern health for the Makepad skills we used this month.
output_examples.output: A concise health report listing successful patterns, failing patterns, review priorities, and recommended maintenance actions.

best_practices: Ask before applying automatic edits to shared skill files or project settings.
Verify Makepad branch and API behavior before documenting a correction.
Keep additions reusable and avoid adding project-specific implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every local workaround as a reusable Makepad pattern.
Do not silently modify shared skill files in a repository without review.
Do not update version numbers locally unless you are handling a maintainer release.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No executable script is packaged. It documents commands and workflows that an agent or user may choose to run.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to read project files and edit skill files automatically. That behavior needs user awareness and approval.

faq.question: Can I use it outside Makepad projects?
faq.answer: It is strongly Makepad-specific. Other projects would need a different skill structure and validation checklist.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I verify before accepting changes?
faq.answer: Check the target skill file, Makepad branch, API behavior, and whether the new guidance is reusable.

faq.question: Does it contact external services?
faq.answer: No automated network behavior was found. The file includes GitHub reference links only.
